retrieved_docs.title: Informatics -- Issues of Concern -- Informatics Fields
retrieved_docs.content: Information Architecture: Information architecture studies the development of successful Web sites, software, intranets, and online communities. Architects structure the information and its presentation in a logical and intuitive way so that information can be successfully used.

retrieved_docs.title: Informatics -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Health informatics is the interprofessional field that studies and pursues the effective uses of biomedical data, information, and knowledge for scientific inquiry, problem-solving, decision making, motivated by efforts to improve human health. In other words, it is the science of information where the information is defined as data with meaning.

retrieved_docs.content: CPTAC Terminology, a specialized biomedical vocabulary, includes the concept of غسيل الكلى غير محدد (unspecified dialysis), indicating that within this terminology set there is a designated term for dialysis procedures that are not otherwise specified. This inclusion supports standardized annotation and reporting of dialysis events when detailed information is unavailable, thereby enhancing consistency in data capture and analysis across proteomic and clinical research contexts.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1756
retrieved_docs.content: The term “اضطراب جنيني” (fetal disorder) is mapped from “متلازمة الضائقة الجنينية” (fetal distress syndrome), indicating that the clinical entity described as fetal distress syndrome corresponds to or is represented by the label fetal disorder in certain contexts. This mapping clarifies terminology, ensuring consistent reference to the same condition across medical documentation and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2230
retrieved_docs.content: Medical databases are clinically associated with a variety of obstetric and perinatal conditions. They contain information on الْتِهابُ المَشيماءِ والسَّلَى, تقلص الحوض, تقلص مخرج الحوض, تقلص مدخل الحوض, قلة السائل الأمنيوسي, نواتج متبقية من الحمل, and gestation twin. By linking these entities, databases support the identification, monitoring, and management of these complications, facilitating evidence‑based decision‑making in maternal‑fetal medicine.

Additionally, the clinically_associated_with relationship connects ct scan abdomen with تصوير البطن بالموجات فوق الصوتية (abdominal ultrasound), reflecting clinical practice where abdominal CT is often considered alongside ultrasound for comprehensive abdominal assessment. Overall, the network of relationships demonstrates a hierarchical and cross‑referential structure for CT imaging of the abdomen, pelvis, chest, and combined regions, distinguishing basic, contrast‑enhanced, and specialized studies, linking them to related imaging modalities, and associating them with standardized diagnostic metadata for patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The medical grouping “الكَلَف” includes as one of its members the SMQ titled “حالات الحمل الطبيعي ونتائجه (SMQ)”, which covers natural pregnancy cases and their outcomes. This relationship indicates that the SMQ for natural pregnancy outcomes is classified within the broader “الكَلَف” category, underscoring its relevance for organizing and interpreting pregnancy‑related clinical information.
